extra.message: User-controlled request.path is interpolated into a string passed to render_template_string(). A crafted 404 URL containing Jinja syntax can be evaluated server-side, enabling SSTI/code injection.

cwe: CWE-1336: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements Used in a Template Engine

extra.message: The Flask secret key is hardcoded. Anyone with source access can forge or tamper with signed Flask session cookies across deployments using this key.

cwe: CWE-321: Use of Hard-coded Cryptographic Key

extra.message: A static registration code is hardcoded and seeded into the database, allowing anyone who knows the code to create an account.
Default user and admin credentials are hardcoded and created on application startup, including an admin account with password 'admin'.
User input from the 'search' query parameter is concatenated into a SQLAlchemy text() SQL fragment with an f-string, allowing SQL injection in the notes search query.
The route uses the user_id URL parameter directly to fetch notes without verifying it matches current_user.id or that the requester is an admin, allowing IDOR access to other users' notes.

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: The account update POST route is missing @login_required even though it modifies account data via current_user, exposing a critical account-management function without authentication enforcement.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: User-controlled form fields are blindly copied into current_user.__dict__. AccountForm includes is_admin, so an authenticated user can submit is_admin to modify their privilege flag.

cwe: CWE-915: Improperly Controlled Modification of Dynamically-Determined Object Attributes

extra.message: Untrusted data from the preferences cookie is base64-decoded and passed to pickle.loads(). A malicious cookie can trigger arbitrary code execution during deserialization.
The registration code supplied by the user is interpolated directly into a raw SQL query passed to session.execute(text(...)), allowing SQL injection during signup token validation.
The delete route fetches a note by user-controlled note_id and deletes it without checking ownership or admin status, allowing users to delete other users' notes.

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: A URL supplied through the profile image form reaches urlopen() without host or scheme restrictions, allowing server-side requests to internal or attacker-controlled services.
The profile image downloader performs urlopen() with no timeout and then response.read() with no size limit, allowing a slow or very large response to consume server resources.
User-supplied note text is rendered with the Jinja safe filter, disabling HTML escaping and allowing stored cross-site scripting when a malicious note is viewed.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: User-supplied note text is rendered through render_table with safe_columns=['text'], allowing stored cross-site scripting in search results.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: User-supplied note text is rendered through render_table with safe_columns=['text'], allowing stored cross-site scripting in the personal notes view.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: SQLAlchemy engine echo logging is enabled, which logs SQL statements and parameters and can expose emails, password hashes, registration codes, and user-supplied note content in logs.

cwe: CWE-532: Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File
